item.title: Once again IMGUR, thank you so much !!!
item.description: I have been submitting my original music to a local awards show for several years now. Once again, nothing much came of it. I am not that bummed though, because every time I post things on here, I get lots of support, and a bunch of fancy accolades. Honestly, the love I get on here is way better than any awards show would give me. So, thanks so much for your continued support Imgur ! Here is song # 62, hot off the press: https://youtu.be/UqmX6ZDMGjw?si=S-SzyvVdA0Qxnbv7

item.title: It's time for Funny Friday! A humor dump to close out the week (Video heavy)
item.description:
As always with my Funny Friday posts, there's a link to some bonus content that I don't put in the main post to avoid getting flagged for no good reason. So make sure you check the comments for that. (Btw, even if you skip some other stuff, I strongly recommend you check out the last video in this dump. You'll know why when you see it.) Also, I don't make these posts every week, so if you like what you see and want to be notified when I do put out on of these posts, let me know in the comments below.
You know Joseph never let that shit go.
... me too. Too much time developing meme brain.
I'm filing a complaint with the Elrick brothers. This is not an equivalent exchange!
The only way this could have been better is if he came back and it was totally demolished like in the Street Fighter bonus stage.
Ever had a moment when you realize that someone you thought was an idiot was actually a genius? (Artist is GRS, you can see more of her work like this on reddit: https://www.reddit.com/user/GRS-/submitted/?sort=top)
Our phones really don't give us any credit at all. If I was going to buy that kind of Coke, first off I wouldn't be leaving any evidence, and second, I wouldn't need a reminder that I was out!
Amazing the things you can learn about yourself and your relationships watching a movie.
Now if only my problems can be solved that easily when I'm awake!
It's "Stuff me Elmo" now.
I've never been as awkward and unsexy as when I was trying to be smooth and sexy.
Sigfried and Roy have never been the same since they turned their act into a road show. (Kudos to that one dude for his commitment to the bit)
Paige...
If she doesn't scare you, no evil thing will.
More breeds and videos from the creator available here: https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PL4FOeRbs2Mb3OD-AiFCZVPVyXc2_JuVwR
Dude. Uncool.
My man is going to be in permanent stun lock mode
The movies would have been much shorter if the moth behaved like the real thing.
This is the greatest tag team since Hogan and Savage.
Someone was having a bad day with typos.
Rule of Acquisition #389: If someone doesn't know the product well, they can be tricked into buying something that only looks like the real thing.
I guess Chris is popular these days! (Or everyone is filling his inbox with trash talk)
Shirley he can't be serious!
Every time the song gets stuck in your head, that's Joe trying to claw his way back into our dimension.
When Willie's done with you, you're going to be saying something stronger than "Oh bother", Pooh.
Ever gone back and forth between manga and comics but your brain starts reading the wrong way and it totally screws with your reading experience?
Stannis Baratheon sends his regards!
This might be too true to count as funny.
100% accurate.
This cat is a reincarnated war criminal. Change my mind. And since I don't have a cat, so this is the closest thing you get to a cat tax. Have a great weekend.
item.dc:creator: politicalwanderer

item.title: Exactly one year ago, the world was shocked by the terrible execution of Oleksandr Matsievskyi. The Ukrainian soldier of the 119th TDF, unarmed, who was captured during the battle of Bakhmut, looked the Russian soldiers in the eyes, took a last drag on hi
item.description: and uttered the legendary words "Slava Ukraini". They then brutally shot Matsievskyi to death with automatic rifles from several sides. Matsievskyi, Hero of Ukraine, symbolizes bravery, the continuous struggle to maintain Ukraine's sovereignty and never allow itself to be subjected to Russian imperialism. Let us not forget Matsievskyi, but also all those others who fell for defending a sovereign Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: vladrichdemaclant

item.title: Celebrating International Women's Day.

item.title: Admiral Grace Hopper was a bigger badass than you'll ever be
item.description:
When the Navy first encountered Grace MF'in Hopper, they said "Ha! You are but a tiny woman!" and rejected her. But she came back. Eventually they let her join the Naval Reserve (Women's Reserve). Then she earned her commission, and that's when things got cool - because the military was in need of some big brains.
That right there is the IBM MARK I computer, from 1944. No, it didn't run DOOM. Data was stored and counted mechanically using 3,000 decimal storage wheels, 1,400 rotary dial switches and 500 miles of wire. On the end of that photo is Grace Hopper. She and a man named Howard Aiken designed the MARK computers (there was a MARK II in 1947) for Harvard.
"Programming" these computers wasn't much like what we do today. Their instructions were almost purely mathematical, and required that numbers be entered in terms a computer could more easily understand, such as using base-8 (octal) digits instead of the normal base 10 (decimal) digits. One of the best stories about Grace has to be about the trouble she had in real life as a result of spending so much time in computer mode: After spending all her days thinking in Octal, every month Hopper's checkbook would be unbalanced, because she would slip into octal addition and subtraction when balancing her accounts. (note - ask your grandparents what "checkbooks" and "balancing" are)
That right there is the first computer bug. I'm not kidding. That is the actual moth which got stuck in the MARK I in 1945, and is *the reason* we use terms like "bug" and "debug". That was Grace. Grace did that. Edit: A commenter made a reasonable argument that the term had been in use before this, and the moth was just the first time an actual bug was involved - which does line up well with the writing on the paper. So - I'll note that here, and leave the rest of the post as-is
She created some of the world's first programming languages (and others were inspired by her creations). She invented debugging. She worked her way up to Admiral and forced a bunch of military brass to re-evaluate what women could do. Grace Hopper Computing Badass 1906-1992 Today's other post about an early computing badass: https://imgur.com/gallery/pTFvmZO
item.dc:creator: Magnebro

item.title: Self-checkout is now a paid subscription
item.description: “If you want to use the self-checkout lane at some Walmart stores, you might need to fork out $12.95 per month, or $98 per year, for a Walmart+ subscription. That's because some Walmart stores are limiting some self-checkout lanes to Walmart+ subscribers and drivers of its Sparks delivery service.” Wait a minute… so we went from making customers checking themselves out so stores didn’t have to pay cashiers, to now charging customers to perform the job that they don’t want to pay employees to do!?!?!
item.dc:creator: NRiNR1

item.title: A dump for your dump
item.dc:creator: originalhuman

item.title: Bringing my 8-year dream of a Paper Plane game to life in a hand-painted world
item.description:
I've wanted to make a Paper Plane game for about 8 years starting from my school years until now being able to finally make it happen!
The core experience of the game revolves around the serene and immersive sensation of gliding through air currents, seamlessly transitioning between moments of calm and exhilarating speed. For those interested in following its progress more closely, the game now has a dedicated Steam page: https://store.steampowered.com/app/1617230/Paper_Sky/
I've been working on this project for 2 years now and I'm saying it's a 8 years dream project of mine because if you look up "Paper Sky" on facebook there's actually a very rough video game project group that I started with a couple friends to try and create what is now what you see.
Paper Sky prioritizes immersive exploration across diverse, seamlessly connected worlds. A completely fluid experience without interruptions like loading screens or pop-ups
Here's a quick demonstration of how one of the mechanics works for the paper plane: when you reach a nearby hole that will densify air flow you are pulled by it and follow a tangent vector (represented here by the default sphere). That way any hard flight tricks become much easier to accomplish giving you both satisfaction and more air momentum
Peace!
item.dc:creator: Anstabo

item.title: It's another unhinged XL Friday CeleryDump for the good & bad people of Imgur
item.description:
greetings online friends
i put a lot of hours into VC but way more into San Andreas
30 is young for a tortoise
because you want a squid fact
yeah that's pretty good
mmm hmm
truly a lobster lover's dream
aka every day
he's right you know
nice, a cooking fork
those talons
i need to watch this
"do you like Phil Collins?"
lemme get a honk on that bobo
that is a work of art
mmm, forbidden chex..
whoa whoa slow down i need to take notes
that's my time.. have a great weekend, gang!!
